<!-- GENERATED by src/sync_canon.py from the vault styleguide. Do not edit by hand; edit the vault docs and rebuild. -->
# Audit Protocol

**AGENTS MUST READ THIS SECTION FIRST!**

This protocol guides a review of Python additions or edits against the styleguide. Read it first, then the section relevant to the code in front of you, and report the violations you find.

Assume the toolchain has already run and passed: Ruff has linted and formatted, and ty has checked the types (see Conventions). Do not raise a finding for anything they enforce or auto-fix. This review covers only the judgment rules they cannot see.

Each rule leads with an RFC 2119 keyword that sets its severity:

| Keyword                     | Severity |
| --------------------------- | -------- |
| **must** / **must not**     | blocker  |
| **should** / **should not** | warning  |
| **may**                     | optional |

Where a rule states only an imperative, map the verb: "never" and "do not" carry the weight of **must not**; "avoid" reads as **should not**; "prefer", "use", and "keep" read as **should**.

Each section carries an `[!info] Audit` callout naming the judgment checks that matter there, the deviations that are acceptable, and what the tooling already covers. Work from those callouts, and never flag a deviation a callout marks acceptable.

For every finding, cite the section and the rule, state the severity, explain the why in one line, and propose a concrete fix modeled on the section's good example. When uncertain, prefer silence over a false positive. Leave formatting, import order, and anything the formatter or linter owns out of the review.

Ignore links and cross-references to the broader Styleguide; everything a review needs is isolated in this protocol and the section file in front of you.
